title: Excavations at Tel Beth-Shean 1989–1996, Volume I
author: Amihai Mazar
publisher: Israel Exploration Society / Hebrew University of Jerusalem
allowedUseNotes: Copyrighted excavation report. Summaries in project's own words only.
summary: Volume I of the renewed Beth-Shean excavations: Mazar's synthesis of the full excavated sequence on the tell from the Late Bronze Age IIB through the medieval period, including the Egyptian garrison town, its decline, and the Iron I town on the summit — the setting for the display of Saul's body in 1 Samuel 31 and for Milestone 3's Beth-shan scene.
confidenceNotes: Volume identity resolved via secondary bibliographic search (queue #16 pass, 2026-07-16): this card is specifically Volume I (2006), the tell-wide Late Bronze IIB-medieval synthesis, not the Iron I-specific Areas N/S strata volume (Volume III, Mazar & Panitz-Cohen 2009 — see mazar-panitz-cohen-2009-beth-shean-vol3) or Mazar's own popular-level synthesis of the fortification/temple/Philistine-presence questions (mazar-2012-bar-saul-impaled), both now added as separate, more precisely relevant cards. Page-level citations within this specific volume are still TO VERIFY by primary-copy inspection; the substantive queue-#16 findings (stratum, wall evidence, monuments, temple identification) are instead cited to the two new cards, which were checkable via secondary summaries during this pass.
